name: imap-smtp-email
tagline: ClawHub skill (107★): reads and sends mail over IMAP/SMTP — checks new mail, fetches and sends messages.
verdict: Falls back to a plaintext password file in its own directory (600 permissions, app-passwords recommended) that never leaves your machine — a real but contained weakness. It also tells you to disable TLS certificate verification for self-signed certs, and its own docs disagree on which config file loads first.

highlights.summary: Config-precedence contradiction between lines 36 and 63 (which .env is checked first).
Advises *_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=false for self-signed certs — weakens TLS verification.
Falls back to a plaintext-password .env in the skill dir (600-perm, app-passwords recommended) — credentials stay local and are never echoed or sent anywhere but the user's own servers.
